dataset: Synthetic expense management data generated by setup_task.sh (50 transactions, 8 employees, 40 approved vendors, 15 project codes)

pi_items.source: Live environment test: extracted full approved vendor list (40 vendors) from localhost:8080/vendors.html. Programmatically confirmed neither 'Pacific Coast Consulting' nor 'Sterling Associates' appears in the approved vendor directory. Evidence file: 04_pattern_verification.txt, 11_vendor_list_complete.txt.

Live environment test: extracted all 50 transactions from localhost:8080/ledger.html JS data array. Filtered for vendor in ['Pacific Coast Consulting','Sterling Associates']. Found exactly 4 matches: TXN-008 (Marcus Johnson, Pacific Coast Consulting, $3200, 2025-10-08), TXN-019 (James O'Brien, Sterling Associates, $4500, 2025-10-22), TXN-031 (Marcus Johnson, Pacific Coast Consulting, $2800, 2025-11-08), TXN-042 (Sarah Chen, Sterling Associates, $1200, 2025-11-19). Evidence: 04_pattern_verification.txt.

Live environment test: grouped all 50 transactions by (vendor, date, amount) tuple. Found exactly 3 groups with >1 entry and different employees: (1) The Capital Grille, 2025-10-17, $186.50 = TXN-014 (Marcus Johnson) + TXN-015 (James O'Brien); (2) Marriott Hotels, 2025-11-03, $245.00 = TXN-025 (Sarah Chen) + TXN-026 (Robert Mitchell); (3) Uber, 2025-11-12, $67.30 = TXN-033 (Priya Patel) + TXN-034 (David Kim). All pairs confirmed to have different employees. Evidence: 04_pattern_verification.txt.

Live environment test: extracted 15 valid project codes (PRJ-001 through PRJ-015) from localhost:8080/projects.html. Cross-referenced all 50 transaction project codes. Found 2 non-existent codes: TXN-022 uses PRJ-099 (not in directory), TXN-038 uses PRJ-087 (not in directory). Found 2 mismatched codes: TXN-027 charges 'Dining' (Client entertainment dinner) to PRJ-003 (Server Infrastructure Upgrade), TXN-044 charges 'Software' (Cloud hosting quarterly) to PRJ-007 (Office Renovation Phase 2). All 4 matches metadata claim. Evidence: 04_pattern_verification.txt.

Live environment test: extracted valid project codes from /projects page — PRJ-001 through PRJ-015 only. PRJ-099 and PRJ-087 are absent from the project directory. TXN-022 (Priya Patel, Starbucks, $34.50) uses PRJ-099. TXN-038 (David Kim, Adobe, $599.00) uses PRJ-087. Evidence: 04_pattern_verification.txt.

metadata_value.TXN-027: Client dinner charged to PRJ-003 (Server Infrastructure Upgrade)
metadata_value.TXN-044: Cloud hosting charged to PRJ-007 (Office Renovation Phase 2)

verified_value.TXN-027: Client entertainment dinner (Dining, $312.00) charged to PRJ-003 (Server Infrastructure Upgrade, IT Operations dept)
verified_value.TXN-044: Cloud hosting quarterly compute charges (Software, $1247.00) charged to PRJ-007 (Office Renovation Phase 2, Facilities dept)

pi_items.source: Live environment test: TXN-027 in ledger = James O'Brien, The Capital Grille, $312.00, category=Dining, project=PRJ-003. Project directory shows PRJ-003 = 'Server Infrastructure Upgrade' (IT Operations dept, $85,000 budget). Clear mismatch: dining expense vs server infrastructure project. TXN-044 in ledger = David Kim, AWS, $1247.00, category=Software, project=PRJ-007. Project directory shows PRJ-007 = 'Office Renovation Phase 2' (Facilities dept, $150,000 budget). Clear mismatch: cloud hosting/software vs office renovation project. Evidence: 04_pattern_verification.txt, 09_projects_page.png.

Computed as union of all violations from Patterns 1-3: shell_company_txns (4) + double_claim entries (6) + budget_code_misuse_txns (4) = 14 unique violation entries. Verified that remaining 36 transactions all use valid project codes (PRJ-001 through PRJ-015), do not use shell company vendors, and have no duplicate (vendor, date, amount) pairs with other employees. Evidence: 04_pattern_verification.txt, 03_full_transaction_data.txt.

Live environment test: Maria Rodriguez has 5 transactions (TXN-005, TXN-013, TXN-024, TXN-037, TXN-047) — all use PRJ-012 (Supply Chain Optimization, valid code), all from approved vendors (Staples, FedEx, Enterprise Rent-A-Car, UPS), none are in any double-claim pair. Lisa Thompson has 4 transactions (TXN-007, TXN-017, TXN-028, TXN-041) — all use PRJ-013 (HR System Migration, valid code), all from approved vendors (Panera Bread, FedEx Office, Chipotle, Amazon Business), none in any double-claim pair. Zero violations for both employees. Evidence: 04_pattern_verification.txt.

Cross-referenced with task description and Desktop investigation brief. Both specify 'Bookmark the Expense Ledger page and the Project Directory page in a Chrome bookmark folder named "Active Investigation"'. The folder name is consistent across task.json description, metadata, and the ~/Desktop/Q4_Investigation_Brief.txt delivered to the agent. Evidence: 02_environment_checks.txt, 16_investigation_brief.txt.

Live environment test: directory exists at /home/ga/Documents/Fraud_Investigation with permissions 755, owner ga:ga. Task description and investigation brief both specify this path. Chrome preferences at task start show download.default_directory is NOT SET (agent must configure it). Evidence: 02_environment_checks.txt, 12_chrome_state.txt.

Cross-referenced with task description ('Compile all findings into ~/Documents/Fraud_Investigation/report.txt') and investigation brief ('Create ~/Documents/Fraud_Investigation/report.txt'). Path is consistent. At task start, the file does NOT exist (verified: 'report.txt: NOT FOUND'). The ga user can write to this directory (verified: WRITE_OK). Export_result.sh reads from this exact path. Evidence: 02_environment_checks.txt, 13_export_result_run.txt.

privileged_info_summary: The expense management system at localhost:8080 contains 50 transactions across 8 employees. There are exactly 14 violation entries across 3 fraud patterns. PATTERN 1 (Shell Companies): 'Pacific Coast Consulting' and 'Sterling Associates' are NOT in the approved vendor directory (which contains 40 vendors). Four transactions reference these shell vendors: TXN-008 (Marcus Johnson, Pacific Coast Consulting, $3,200.00, 2025-10-08), TXN-019 (James O'Brien, Sterling Associates, $4,500.00, 2025-10-22), TXN-031 (Marcus Johnson, Pacific Coast Consulting, $2,800.00, 2025-11-08), TXN-042 (Sarah Chen, Sterling Associates, $1,200.00, 2025-11-19). PATTERN 2 (Double Claims): Three pairs of transactions where two different employees submitted matching vendor+date+amount: TXN-014 (Marcus Johnson) + TXN-015 (James O'Brien) — The Capital Grille, 2025-10-17, $186.50; TXN-025 (Sarah Chen) + TXN-026 (Robert Mitchell) — Marriott Hotels, 2025-11-03, $245.00; TXN-033 (Priya Patel) + TXN-034 (David Kim) — Uber, 2025-11-12, $67.30. PATTERN 3 (Budget Code Misuse): Two non-existent project codes — TXN-022 (Priya Patel, Starbucks, $34.50) uses PRJ-099, TXN-038 (David Kim, Adobe, $599.00) uses PRJ-087; neither code exists in the project directory (PRJ-001 through PRJ-015 only). Two mismatched project codes — TXN-027 (James O'Brien, The Capital Grille, $312.00, Dining) charged to PRJ-003 which is 'Server Infrastructure Upgrade' (IT Operations), TXN-044 (David Kim, AWS, $1,247.00, Software) charged to PRJ-007 which is 'Office Renovation Phase 2' (Facilities). Maria Rodriguez (5 transactions) and Lisa Thompson (4 transactions) have ZERO violations. The agent must create report.txt at ~/Documents/Fraud_Investigation/report.txt, create a bookmark folder named 'Active Investigation' containing bookmarks for the Expense Ledger and Project Directory pages, and set Chrome's download directory to /home/ga/Documents/Fraud_Investigation. The project codes are only visible by clicking into transaction detail rows (not in the summary table), which is essential for detecting Pattern 3. The ledger is paginated with 10 entries per page (5 pages total).
